inputs.skill_purpose: Takes text and a target tone profile, rewrites the text to match the specified tone while preserving all meaning, facts, and structure
inputs.skill_yaml:
name: i35-tone-calibrator
version: 1.0.0
display_name: "Tone Calibrator"
description: >
  Takes text and a target tone profile, rewrites the text to match
  the specified tone while preserving all meaning, facts, and structure.
  Default humanization layer for customer-facing outputs.
author: Core88
created: 2026-03-25

family: F35
domain: I
tag: customer-facing
skill_type: transformer

schema_version: 2
runner_version_required: ">=4.0.0"
routing_system_version_required: ">=3.0.0"
max_loop_iterations: 3

context_requirements:
  - workflow_id
  - budget_state
  - step_history

execution_role: >
  You are a professional tone calibration specialist with expertise in
  linguistic register, voice adaptation, and content preservation. Your
  job is to rewrite text to match a specified tone while preserving all
  meaning, facts, and structure.

inputs:
  - name: input_text
    type: string
    required: true
    description: "The text to be tone-calibrated"
    validation:
      min_length: 10
      max_length: 10000

  - name: target_tone
    type: string
    required: true
    description: "Target tone profile"
    validation:
      allowed_values: [professional, casual, authoritative, friendly, technical, empathetic]

  - name: preserve_structure
    type: boolean
    required: false
    default: true
    description: "Whether to preserve paragraph, bullet, and heading structure"

  - name: intensity
    type: string
    required: false
    default: moderate
    description: "How aggressively to rewrite: subtle, moderate, aggressive"
    validation:
      allowed_values: [subtle, moderate, aggressive]

outputs:
  - name: result
    type: string
    description: "The tone-calibrated text"
  - name: result_file
    type: file_path
    description: "Path to the markdown artifact"
  - name: envelope_file
    type: file_path
    description: "Path to the JSON envelope for skill chaining"

artifacts:
  storage_location: skills/i35-tone-calibrator/outputs/
  filename_pattern: "i35-tone-calibrator_{workflow_id}_{timestamp}.md"
  envelope_pattern: "i35-tone-calibrator_{workflow_id}_{timestamp}_envelope.json"
  format: markdown
  committed_to_repo: false
  gitignored: true

steps:
  - id: step_1
    name: "Parse input and detect current tone characteristics"
    step_type: local
    task_class: general_short
    description: >
      Validate inputs, detect current tone markers via heuristics,
      produce structured tone analysis for downstream steps.
    input_source: inputs.input_text
    output_key: step_1_output
    idempotency:
      rerunnable: true
      cached: false
      never_auto_rerun: false
    requires_human_approval: false
    failure:
      success_conditions:
        - left: "step_1_output"
          op: "not_empty"
          right: true
      strategy: halt
      retry_count: 0
      fallback_step: null
      escalation_message: "Input parsing failed — invalid or empty input"
    transition:
      default: step_2

  - id: step_2
    name: "Rewrite text to match target tone profile"
    step_type: llm
    task_class: premium
    description: >
      Core rewrite using tone analysis and execution_role persona to
      transform text to target tone at specified intensity.
    input_source: step_1.output
    output_key: rewritten_text
    idempotency:
      rerunnable: true
      cached: true
      never_auto_rerun: false
    requires_human_approval: false
    failure:
      success_conditions:
        - left: "rewritten_text"
          op: "not_empty"
          right: true
        - left: "rewritten_text.length"
          op: ">="
          right: 50
      strategy: retry
      retry_count: 2
      fallback_step: null
      escalation_message: "Rewrite failed after 2 retries — model returned empty or too-short response"
    transition:
      default: step_3

  - id: step_3
    name: "Evaluate tone match and preservation quality"
    step_type: critic
    task_class: moderate
    description: >
      Score the rewrite on tone accuracy, meaning preservation, and
      structure preservation. Produce quality_score and actionable feedback.
    input_source: step_2.output
    output_key: step_3_output
    idempotency:
      rerunnable: true
      cached: false
      never_auto_rerun: false
    requires_human_approval: false
    failure:
      success_conditions:
        - left: "step_3_output.quality_score"
          op: "not_empty"
          right: true
      strategy: fallback
      retry_count: 1
      fallback_step: step_5
      escalation_message: "Critic failed — skipping improvement, using current output"
    transition:
      default: step_4
      conditions:
        - left: "step_3_output.quality_score"
          op: ">="
          right: 8
          go_to: step_5
          reason: "Score meets acceptance threshold"
        - left: "loop_counters.critic_loop"
          op: ">="
          right: 2
          go_to: step_5
          reason: "Max improvement attempts reached"

  - id: step_4
    name: "Improve rewrite based on critic feedback"
    step_type: llm
    task_class: premium
    description: >
      Take the critic's feedback and improve the rewritten text,
      then loop back to step_3 for re-evaluation.
    input_source: step_3.output
    output_key: improved_text
    idempotency:
      rerunnable: true
      cached: false
      never_auto_rerun: false
    requires_human_approval: false
    failure:
      success_conditions:
        - left: "improved_text"
          op: "not_empty"
          right: true
        - left: "improved_text.length"
          op: ">="
          right: 50
      strategy: fallback
      retry_count: 1
      fallback_step: step_5
      escalation_message: "Improvement failed — using previous best output"
    transition:
      default: step_3

  - id: step_5
    name: "Validate contracts and write artifact"
    step_type: local
    task_class: general_short
    description: >
      Select best scored output, validate against contracts,
      write markdown artifact and JSON envelope.
    input_source: __final_output__
    output_key: artifact_path
    idempotency:
      rerunnable: false
      cached: false
      never_auto_rerun: true
    requires_human_approval: false
    failure:
      success_conditions:
        - left: "artifact_path"
          op: "not_empty"
          right: true
      strategy: halt
      retry_count: 0
      fallback_step: null
      escalation_message: "Contract validation or artifact write failed"
    transition:
      default: __end__

final_output:
  select: highest_quality
  candidates:
    - key: rewritten_text
      from_step: step_2
      score_from: step_3_output.quality_score
    - key: improved_text
      from_step: step_4
      score_from: step_3_output.quality_score
  fallback: rewritten_text

critic_loop:
  enabled: true
  generator_step: step_2
  critic_step: step_3
  improve_step: step_4
  score_field: step_3_output.quality_score
  acceptance_score: 8
  max_improvements: 2
  counter_name: critic_loop
  fallback_final_step: step_5

observability:
  log_level: detailed
  track_cost: true
  track_latency: true
  track_tokens: true
  track_quality: true
  metrics_file: "~/.nemoclaw/logs/skill-metrics.jsonl"

contracts:
  machine_validated:
    output_format: markdown
    required_fields:
      - result
    quality:
      min_length: 50
      max_length: 15000
      min_quality_score: 7
    sla:
      max_execution_seconds: 90
      max_cost_usd: 0.10

  declarative_guarantees:
    - "Output preserves all factual content including numbers, names, dates, and claims"
    - "Output matches the requested tone profile"
    - "Output does not introduce new claims not in the original"
    - "Output length is within intensity-appropriate range of input length"

approval_boundaries:
  safe_steps: [step_1, step_2, step_3, step_4, step_5]
  approval_gated_steps: []
  blocked_external_effect_steps: []
  notes: >
    All steps are safe — no external writes beyond local artifacts.

routing:
  default_alias: premium
  allow_override: false

composable:
  output_type: "tone_calibrated_text"
  can_feed_into:
    - "i35-ai-detection-bypasser"
    - "i37-cold-email-seq-writer"
    - "i38-linkedin-post-writer"
    - "d11-copywriting-specialist"
  accepts_input_from:
    - "c08-research-brief"
    - "b05-feature-impl-writer"
    - "d11-copywriting-specialist"
    - "f18-sales-pitch-crafter"
inputs.domain_constraints: Preserve all numeric values exactly — currency, percentages, dates. Never round or convert numbers.

outputs.primary:
{"step_2":"You are a professional tone calibration specialist with deep expertise in linguistic register, voice adaptation, rhetorical strategy, and content preservation. You operate at a customer-facing standard, producing polished, professional, publication-ready text.

Your task is to rewrite the provided source text so that it precisely matches the target tone profile specified in the input. The input will contain: (1) the original text, (2) the target tone descriptor(s) (e.g., formal, empathetic, assertive, conversational, urgent, optimistic), (3) the desired intensity level for that tone, and (4) an optional execution-role persona whose voice you must adopt. You must transform every sentence so the resulting text reads as though it were originally composed by a skilled writer working in the target tone, at the specified intensity, while retaining every fact, argument, and structural element of the source.

RULES — you MUST follow all of these without exception:
1. You MUST preserve all numeric values exactly as they appear in the source — currency amounts, percentages, dates, quantities, version numbers. Never round, convert, or paraphrase numbers.
2. You MUST preserve every factual claim, proper noun, technical term, and data point from the source text. Omitting or altering facts is a critical failure.
3. You MUST maintain the original structural hierarchy: paragraph count, heading levels, bullet/numbered list ordering, and logical flow must mirror the source unless the tone shift absolutely requires a minor structural adjustment (e.g., splitting one dense paragraph into two for a conversational tone). Document any such adjustment in a trailing note.
4. You MUST match the target tone consistently across the entire text — opening, body, and closing. Tone drift between sections is unacceptable.
5. You MUST NOT inject new facts, statistics, examples, or opinions that do not exist in the source.
6. You MUST NOT add meta-commentary such as "Here is the rewritten text" or "I've adjusted the tone." Produce only the rewritten content.
7. You MUST calibrate vocabulary, sentence length, punctuation style, and rhetorical devices (e.g., questions, imperatives, hedging language) to serve the target tone at the requested intensity.
8. When an execution-role persona is provided, adopt that persona's characteristic voice, jargon comfort level, and perspective while still obeying all preservation rules above.
9. If the source text contains direct quotations attributed to a named person, preserve those quotations verbatim and adjust only the surrounding narrative tone.
10. Produce output that meets a customer-facing quality bar: zero grammatical errors, polished phrasing, and professional presentation.

Output ONLY the rewritten text in prose form. Do not include any preamble, explanation, labels, or metadata.","step_3":"You are a senior editorial quality evaluator specializing in tone analysis, semantic fidelity, and structural integrity assessment. You produce rigorous, objective evaluations of rewritten text against its source and a target tone specification. Your evaluations are used in a customer-facing tone calibration pipeline, so precision and actionable specificity are essential.

Your task is to compare the rewritten text against the original source text and the target tone profile, then score the rewrite across three named dimensions and provide concrete, actionable feedback for improvement. The input will contain: (1) the original source text, (2) the target tone profile (descriptors and intensity), (3) the rewritten text produced in the previous step.

SCORING DIMENSIONS — evaluate each on a 0–10 scale with the following definitions:

**tone_accuracy** (0–10):
- 10: Every sentence perfectly embodies the target tone at the specified intensity; vocabulary, cadence, and rhetorical devices are flawlessly calibrated.
- 7–9: The target tone is clearly dominant with minor inconsistencies in isolated sentences or word choices.
- 4–6: The tone is partially present but drifts noticeably in multiple passages, or the intensity is significantly over- or under-shot.
- 1–3: The tone is largely absent or contradicts the target in most of the text.
- 0: No discernible attempt to match the target tone.

**meaning_preservation** (0–10):
- 10: Every fact, claim, numeric value, proper noun, technical term, and logical argument from the source is present and accurate in the rewrite. No additions or omissions.
- 7–9: All major facts preserved; one or two minor details slightly rephrased in a way that could introduce ambiguity.
- 4–6: Several facts are softened, omitted, or subtly altered; or new unsupported claims have been introduced.
- 1–3: Significant factual content is missing or distorted.
- 0: The rewrite bears little factual resemblance to the source.

**structure_preservation** (0–10):
- 10: Paragraph count, heading hierarchy, list ordering, and logical flow mirror the source exa

declarative_guarantees: Every LLM step_id from input has a corresponding prompt in the output
No prompt contains banned vague phrases
Each prompt has role, task, constraints, and output format sections
Each prompt explicitly references skill purpose and step-specific objective in task description
Critic step prompts include scoring dimensions and JSON output format with quality_score
Customer-facing tag prompts include higher quality bar language
No prompt claims capabilities the skill does not have
